question_text: Why do high heels sink into soft ground but flat shoes do not, even when worn by people of the same weight?
correct_answer: High heels have a much smaller contact area with the ground than flat shoes. The same weight (force) is spread over a smaller area in heels, creating higher pressure that pushes into the soft ground.
explanation: Both people have the same weight, but the high heel concentrates that weight onto a tiny area, creating very high pressure. Flat shoes spread the weight over a larger area, producing lower pressure that the ground can support.
